name: Perrine Lievens
bio: perrine lievens is a french visual artist based in paris. her work uses familiar objects, challenging their perceived function to create imaginary and poetic realities. often fragile and ephemeral, her sculptural installations explore resistance through retaining delicate material qualities. gave an artist presentation at a. farm at MoT+++ in june 2018.

name: Celina Huỳnh
bio: celina huỳnh is a vietnamese-american artist working across performance, sound, and installation. she developed 'seeking severance' during her residency with echo correspondence in vienna, a project centered on the musical legacy of paris by night and its role in shaping vietnamese diaspora identity. she was a resident at a. farm in 2025.

name: Dao Tung
bio: dao tung (b. 1983, ho chi minh city) works with sound, installation, and video, drawing from his practice as a music composer and sound artist. his practice engages with urban society, political issues, and identity. a graduate of the university of economics in ho chi minh city, he has collaborated on performances in vietnam, korea, and the usa. his video work 'it seems to be' was the inaugural work in MoT+++'s +1 museum by any other name. in 2017 he participated in an artist exchange at 18th street arts center in los angeles.

name: Lê Hiền Minh
bio: lê hiền minh is a vietnamese visual artist whose practice centers on material transformation and cultural memory. working with traditional vietnamese dó paper, she creates works that explore the poetic and tactile properties of craft and handmade materials. her work 'hạt' was presented at MoT+++ in the 2016 group exhibition 'kệ.' in 2017, she collaborated with cam xanh on 'home | land,' presented at the taiwan annual art fair.

name: Lại Diệu Hà
bio: lại diệu hà is a hanoi-based interdisciplinary artist and founder of psyper lab, a performance art collective. her practice spans performance, painting, installation, and video, with recurring themes of sexuality, the body, and personal and collective memory. known for her acclaimed performance 'fly away' at nha san collective (2010). in 2018, she completed a residency at MoT+++ as part of the six-month performance plus program.

name: Le Quoc Thanh
bio: le quoc thanh is a ho chi minh city-based artist who participated in an exchange residency with baik art in seoul, south korea (november – december 2018), as part of MoT+++'s ongoing exchange collaboration with baik art.

name: Maxime Brygo
bio: maxime brygo is a french photographer who completed a three-month residency at a. farm (season 0) in ho chi minh city in 2018. his photographic work documented southern vietnamese landscapes and monuments. his prints were exhibited at the a. farm season 0 closing exhibition 'buy . bay . buy' (december 2018).

name: Nguyễn Thuỷ Tiên
bio: nguyễn thuỷ tiên (b. 1993, hanoi) is a vietnamese artist living and working between hanoi and frankfurt am main. her practice centers on the transformation of personal and collective memory — how it is distorted, translated, and reconstructed through time — beginning with the properties of everyday objects and their narrative associations within societal codes. she holds a BA from the hanoi academy of theater and cinema (2015) and graduated as meisterschülerin from the städelschule in frankfurt, where she studied with haegue yang (2018–2024). her work has been shown internationally at documenta 15 (kassel), delfina foundation (london), RAY triennale (frankfurt), and nova contemporary (bangkok). participated in MoT+++'s performance plus program (2018).

name: Phương Giò
bio: phương giò was a resident artist at a. farm during season 0 (2018), the first season of MoT+++'s artist residency program in ho chi minh city. her work was presented at the season 0 closing exhibition 'buy . bay . buy' (december 2018).

name: Aliansyah Caniago
bio: an interdisciplinary artist whose work critically examines the intersections of power, social injustice, and environmental crises. he completed a Master of Arts in Art and Ecology from Goldsmiths, University of London. his practice engages with the idea of central (metropole) and periphery (satellite) and works with communities affected by industrialisation in Java and Sumatera, through studio-based practices, site-specific installations, and durational performances exploring land-use conflict, migration, urbanisation, waste, and contested spaces. he investigates Barus, a North Sumatran port town connected to his family history and the extinction of the Camphor Tree. co-founder of Unground Collective (London), Gerilya Artist Collective (Bandung). exhibitions include Tainan Art Museum (2024), MuseumQuartier (2024), MUMA-Monash University Museum of Art (2018), Jakarta Biennale (2017), 14th Lyon Biennale (2017), Documenta 15 (2022).

name: Arnont Nongyao
bio: arnont nongyao (b. 1979, bangkok) is a thai artist based between chiang mai and ho chi minh city. trained in painting at chiang mai university (BFA, 2003), he now works across sound, video, installation, and public art. his primary research centres on vibration — how it links humans, objects, and society — and his works emphasise participation and modes of listening in social situations. his most important teacher was khvay loeung (1940–2020), the cambodian sound inventor who inspired him to build DIY instruments and engage deeply with listening. he is co-director of the chiang mai collective (CMC), a collective of sound artists and musicians working on experimental sound art projects. his work has been shown at the bangkok art biennale, avatar (quebec), and nusasonic. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus program.

name: Ayumi Adachi
bio: ayumi adachi (b. 1972, hyogo, japan) is a hong kong-based japanese artist who has lived in hong kong since 1996. her work ranges from large-scale immersive installations to individual paintings on shaped mirrors, using low-technology materials to create site- and time-specific art. her practice is guided by the axis of time and the life cycles of nature and humans — concepts she expresses through 'setsuna' (circular time theory). recent sculptural work titled 'hare ke' constructs this time theory through traditional japanese views of temporality. solo and group exhibitions since 1992 in japan, macau, taiwan, shanghai, and hong kong. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Bang Nhat Linh
bio: bàng nhất linh (b. 1983, hanoi) is a vietnamese contemporary artist whose practice probes memory, history, and the depths of psychological space. born in khâm thiên — a neighbourhood deeply marked by the vietnam war — he developed an early interest in war memorabilia, which he collects and repositions within new contexts to create interactive, communicable objects. his works are often simple yet multi-layered, dreamlike and humane. notable projects include 'the vacant chair' (2015), shown at MoT+++, nha san collective, and the mori art museum as part of 'sunshower: contemporary art from southeast asia 1980s to now' (2017), and 'summer grasses' (2018). he graduated from the vietnam university of fine arts (2005) and is affiliated with the nguyen art foundation and post vidai collection.

name: Bert Nguyễn San
bio: Bert Nguyễn San is a Ho Chi Minh City-based artist and writer who participated in A. Farm and MoT+++ programmes.

name: Bill Nguyen
bio: a Ho Chi Minh City-based artist and curator whose practice spans performance, curation, and conceptual art. he performed in performance plus 2018 and 2019 at MoT+++, including the durational piece Bless You Hold Me (with Cam Xanh) and curated the Happy Birthday Bill programme.

name: Cam Xanh
bio: Cam Xanh (real name: Tran Thanh Ha, b.1977) is a conceptual artist from Vietnam based in Saigon (Ho Chi Minh City). she co-founded the Post Vidai Collection — the first collection dedicated to contemporary Vietnamese art — in 2004 and has acted as its Director / Curator since then. in 2014 she co-founded the Dia Projects which morphed into MoT+++. she co-founded the MoT collective, a.farm, and the Nguyen Art Foundation. she began making art in 2012 under the pseudonym Cam Xanh and has exhibited in Vietnam and internationally. her artist name Cam Xanh, which translates as Green Orange, exemplifies the minimalist and playful nature of her works.

name: Nguyễn Chung
bio: nguyễn chung is a dance artist, somatic movement facilitator, and biodynamic craniosacral therapist based in melbourne/naarm. a graduate of the vietnam dance academy in hanoi, they worked from 2009–2016 as a full-time dancer with arabesque dance company (vietnam), t.h.e dance company (singapore), and cinevox junior company (switzerland), winning a special prize at the korea international modern dance competition (2013). their practice integrates somatic and contemplative approaches to healing and performance — spanning dance, movement, therapeutic touch, sleep, and dream. they hold a diploma in biodynamic craniosacral therapy and are pursuing additional study in somatic movement education. performances have been presented across asia, europe, the americas, and australia, including at MoT+++ (performance plus 2019), para||el performance space in brooklyn, and american dance festival.

name: Cian Duggan
bio: explores relationships between human and non-human elements, temporal and eternal concepts. his work questions presence, absence, and temporal structures while examining connections between natural and supernatural forces. his ongoing series Mediums (2020—ongoing) features a recurring "universal form" that transcends specific time periods and spatial boundaries. works are UV printed onto plexiglass that shifts appearance based on light, space, and the presence of people and animals. the practice views time, environment, the living, and the non-living as all in a constant state of flux.

name: Đặng Hồng Anh
bio: đặng hồng anh is a self-taught vietnamese artist whose works explore the personal and non-personal — dreams, imagination, and the spaces between. presenting observations and reflections, her practice poses questions and aims to provoke. she is associated with á space (experimental arts) in vietnam. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus program.

name: Dang Thuy Anh
bio: đặng thùy anh was born in hanoi and lives and works in ho chi minh city. she graduated from vietnam university of fine arts in 2019, majoring in graphic design. since 2017 she has engaged in experimental practice through performance art, installation, photography, and conceptual approaches. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Đỗ Nguyễn Lập Xuân
bio: đỗ nguyễn lập xuân (b. 1986, ho chi minh city) is an artist working across sculpture, drawing, installation, performance, and time-based media. her practice is underpinned by belief in the intangible connections between people; her work explores the tacit ambiguity of selfhood as it operates in both mind and environment, inviting viewer participation in sensory experiences that open intimate dialogues. she is a scientia phd candidate at the university of new south wales (unsw sydney), where her research explores 'voices' across themes of liminality, identity, displacement, and marginalisation. she has exhibited at sàn art and the factory contemporary arts centre in ho chi minh city. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus program.

name: Doan Thanh Toan
bio: toàn (he/they) is a choreographer and movement artist based in ho chi minh city. born in quang ngai, he trained in yorchha™ contemporary indian technique with ananya dance theater (minneapolis) and african-based movement with brown spirit ensemble. his choreographic work engages vietnamese socio-political contexts and queer identity, exploring the imprint of typhoons and displacement on central vietnamese psyches. in 2024 he co-founded project đẩy sàn, a grassroots platform for experimental performance, alongside producer nguyễn hải yến, writer nhật huỳnh-vũ, and theatre-maker trà nguyễn. recent works include the ensemble piece 'chiêm bao thấy mình' (2022) and the movement-based film 'buoyant / bồng bềnh' (2023) with filmmaker nguyễn lê hoàng phúc. he has created work at MoT+++, sàn art, a.farm, time between, and goethe-institut. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Emmanuelle Huynh
bio: emmanuelle huynh is a french dancer, choreographer, and educator of vietnamese heritage. trained in both philosophy and dance, she received a villa médicis hors-les-murs grant to travel to vietnam in 1994 — an experience that shaped her landmark solo múa (1995), whose title means 'dance' in vietnamese. the work charts an internal journey at the frontier of darkness, tracing a body caught between light and shadow, interiority and exterior, vietnam and france. her practice explores relationships with literature, music, light, and architecture, combining contemporary dance, music, and visual arts across disciplines. she directed the centre national de danse contemporaine in angers (2004–2012) and since 2016 has led an atelier at the école des beaux-arts in paris. she presented dusk dance for the saigon river at MoT+++ in february 2020.

name: Flinh
bio: flinh (nguyen phuong linh, b. 1995, hanoi) is a visual artist working across installation, video, and performance. she studied painting at the university of fine art (hanoi) and has participated in in:act festivals, nipaf international 2018 (japan), and asia live performance 2018 (poland). participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Hoàng Minh Đức
bio: hoàng minh đức is a hanoi-based performance artist and co-founder of phụ lục (the appendix group), a performance collective established in 2010. phụ lục is known for durational, repetitive performances employing allegorical everyday objects in abstractly staged settings, using minimal gestures to evoke social concerns. the group participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2018 residency.

name: Hoang Nam Viet
bio:
Hoang Nam Viet (b. 1985, Ho Chi Minh City) is a self-taught painter and multidisciplinary artist. After working in advertising, he began painting oils at 28 and committed fully to his practice at 30. He is also the founder of Hoàng Thị café, a beloved creative hub in Ho Chi Minh City.

His paintings center on the people closest to him — friends, colleagues, familiar faces — rendered with candor and warmth. Broadly expressionist in sensibility, his work resists sentimentality while affirming the depth of everyday human connection. Recent works incorporate found imagery, expanding his visual vocabulary beyond portraiture.

Hoang Nam Viet has exhibited widely across Ho Chi Minh City's contemporary art scene, including Password 0~1 at MoT+++; Lunar Breccia and Hopscotch at Galerie Quynh; The Primacy of Drawing at Dia Projects; Chung at PongDang Gallery; and Out of Nowhere at Sao La. He is represented by Galerie Quynh and lives and works in Ho Chi Minh City.

name: Kim Duy
bio: Kim Duy was born in Hanoi, Vietnam in 1987. Duy's artistic practice experimented with a variety of objects and materials such as printers, ballpoint pens, markers, etc. to more traditional materials such as pencil and paper. his work explores themes of time, space, history, and the performative nature of still images through a multidisciplinary practice. he works with modest materials like paper and uses text as an allegory for how information is transformed through individual subjectivity. the work avoids direct political commentary and instead examines what art can accomplish in society through a focus on art-making processes.

name: Le Phi Long
bio: Le Phi Long, born in 1988, is a visual artist based in Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam. he graduated in interior design from Hue Fine Art University in 2012. Long's art encompasses paintings, site-specific installations, and conceptual works, deeply rooted in historical and geographical insights. he examines survival, cultural transformation, and their impact on society, particularly his generation, exploring global and local narratives through geography, history, and religion. his Dong Duong Lang Du project (since 2016) examines French Indochina's history regarding identity and migration. co-founded eNAME Art Center in Hanoi (2012) and Lamvien art center in Dalat (2017). residencies include Sàn Art Vietnam (2013), Bamboo Curtain Studio Taiwan (2015), Asian Highway Project Korea (2018), Cité Internationale des Arts Paris (2020). MoT collective member since 2020.

name: Lê Phương Nhi
bio: lê phương nhi (also known as sam) is a performance and installation artist based in saigon. her practice explores themes of natural self, space, and time through familiar materials and objects with potentially metaphorical meanings, retaining the human element and focusing on abstract and organic forms to create intimate spaces for inner reflection. notable works include the performance first and last time (italy, 2019), the installation nonexistent secrets (vietnam, 2020), and the ongoing lifetime project where do they go? (vietnam, 2019–). she has completed a residency at rimbun dahan (malaysia) and is affiliated with sàn art. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus program.

name: Linh Lê
bio: linh lê is an independent curator, writer, and researcher from ho chi minh city. her work investigates the changing landscapes and ecologies of saigon under modernisation, while exploring gaps in contemporary vietnamese art history — particularly in performance and video art. she previously held a curatorial position at galerie quynh. since mid-2024 she has been directing đo đạc, a site-responsive curatorial project surveying the impact of forced resettlement in the thủ thiêm peninsula. she is a curatorial board member of á space (hanoi) and a research fellow of artsEquator's southeast asian arts censorship database project. she curated a duo exhibition of lê đ. chung and tâm đỗ at MoT+++ and is the curator of a. farm.

name: Lyon Nguyễn
bio: Lyon Nguyen is a choreographer based in Ho Chi Minh City. he presented Performance, Presentation & Discussion at A. Farm in January 2025, collaborating with resident artist Ru Marshall on the project Interlope, which investigates the meaning of gesture in visual arts and dance.

name: Lê Mai Anh
bio: a Vietnamese dancer and choreographer, graduate of Rencontres Internationales de Danse Contemporaine in Paris. she presents conceptual performances incorporating dance and poetry. she collaborated with Cam Xanh on sàn!, a performance presented at MoT+++ in 2018.

name: Maria Sowter
bio: maria sowter (b. 1991, london) is a british artist and researcher based between london and ho chi minh city since 2015. her practice is performative and research-based, concerned with how knowledge circulates and is embodied. in 2017 she founded bay library saigon — a poetry, art, zine, and critical theory lending library that began as a travelling exhibition bringing artist books and films from vietnam to european audiences. now housed at MoT+++, the library can be accessed through its saigon book delivery service. she has worked in writing, curatorial, and organizational roles with MoT+++, a.farm, and the nguyen art foundation. BA from queen mary university of london; MA from utrecht university. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Masayuki Miyaji
bio: masayuki miyaji (b. 1985, mie, japan) completed a master of fine arts at tokyo university of the arts. his practice, rooted in sculpture and expanded through media theory, is guided by the idea that 'possibility and impossibility are two sides of the same coin.' he participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Mathieu Dufourg
bio: mathieu dufourg is a french actor, poet, and director based in ho chi minh city. he co-founded the theatre company nés sous x and works across performance, poetry, and cross-cultural production. he authored a critical volume on the work of artist bao vuong, published by skira paris. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Matthew Brannon
bio: Matthew Brannon (b. 1971, St. Maries, Idaho) is recognized for letterpress and screen prints combining images and text. his work employs traditional silkscreen techniques and hand-painting, presenting a stripped-down aesthetic evoking mass production and marketing design. his artistic practice extends across painting, sculpture, video, and installation. since 2015 he has developed an ongoing research project titled Concerning Vietnam exploring the Vietnam/American War. works appear in the Hammer Museum (Los Angeles), Whitney Museum of American Art (New York), and Museum of Modern Art (New York). exhibitions internationally across Belgium, Canada, Germany, Italy, Japan, Mexico, Norway, Spain, Switzerland, UK, and USA. B.A. from University of California Los Angeles; M.F.A. from Columbia University New York. joined MoT+++ collective in 2020. based in New York.

name: Montez Press
bio: Montez Press Radio is a New York-based radio platform and publishing house. they hosted A Neighbor Is A Camera/Canvas Powered By Rice, a series of conversations about collectivity in clubbing and art-making in Vietnam, in collaboration with A. Farm in February 2025.

name: Nam Thi
bio: nam thi is a hanoi-born poet and interdisciplinary artist. he graduated from the academy of journalism and communication with a major in philosophy and works as an editor at l'officiel vietnam. his artistic practice treats poetry as a sanctuary of the mind, using highly symbolic and visually charged language to explore inner life and the imagination. he is associated with the factory contemporary arts centre in ho chi minh city.

name: Ngô Thành Bắc
bio: Ngô Thành Bắc is a Hanoi-based performance artist and core member of Phụ Lục (The Appendix Group), founded in 2010. Phụ Lục served as artists-in-residence throughout the six-month performance plus 2018 programme at MoT+++.

name: Nhân Phan
bio: Nhân Phan is a Ho Chi Minh City-based artist and co-founder of CodeSurfing (Lướt Code), a creative tech collective that situates technology within artistic, research, and archiving practices through poetical approaches. he presented the talk Codesurfing: A Search for a Poetical Technology at A. Farm in 2025.

name: Phụ Lục
bio: phụ lục (the appendix) is a hanoi-based performance art collective founded in 2010 by six artists: nguyễn huy an, vũ đức toàn, ngô thành bắc, nguyễn văn song, hoàng minh đức, and nguyễn dương hải đăng; currently four core members are active. known for durational, repetitive performances employing allegorical everyday objects in abstractly staged settings, the group uses minimal yet potent gestures to evoke social issues and personal concerns. their practice has shifted over time from tightly structured works toward more situational, chance-based approaches. they have performed at festivals across vietnam, singapore, china, and poland. phụ lục served as artists-in-residence throughout the six-month performance plus 2018 programme at MoT+++.

name: Quoc Anh Le
bio:
Quoc Anh Le (b. 1999, Vietnam) is a painter whose work explores the emotional terrain of love, loss, and memory. Working with a minimalist visual language, his paintings carry an intensity of feeling that belies their restraint — vulnerability rendered as form.

His series Eros, Erotoropia, Philautia, Philia, Pragma, Storge and Agápe, shown at MoT+++, maps the Greek taxonomy of love through the lens of personal experience, paying tribute to a lost relationship while connecting with a broader human condition. When in the studio, he is in the company of his pets. He has participated in group exhibitions at MoT+++, Ho Chi Minh City, where he lives and works.

name: Ru Marshall
bio: ru marshall is a nonbinary american artist and writer. their painting practice centers on mirrored surfaces and translucency, exploring identity, memory, and queer experience. their debut novel was lambda literary award-nominated; a biography of carlos castaneda is forthcoming from OR books in 2026. they participated in MoT+++'s performance plus program and were a resident at a.Farm.

name: Samira Jamouchi
bio: samira jamouchi is an interdisciplinary artist, researcher, and associate professor in visual arts. born and raised in brussels to moroccan parents, now based in norway. her practice spans soft sculpture, immersive installations, and performative processes, often working with wool felting as material-discourse — exploring the entanglement of bodies, fibres, and inherited craft traditions. her research interests include moroccan and african migration to europe and the foreign presence in morocco. she holds degrees from the académie royale des beaux-arts (brussels) and the national art academy of oslo. affiliated with østfold university college, her work has been published in journals addressing art-based pedagogy and a/r/tography. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Shayekh Mohammed Arif
bio: shayekh mohammad arif is a dhaka-based sound artist and composer. his sound draws from trance, spirituality, and ethnicity. presented at birmingham weekender festival (2017), transforming narratives uk (2019), landgoed leonardus art residency netherlands (2019). south asian music residency 2019 resident. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Sikarnt Skoolisariyaporn
bio: sikarnt skoolisariyaporn is an artist, writer, and filmmaker based between london and bangkok. their interdisciplinary practice combines writing, performance, installation, and moving image to explore the politics of memory and the re-imagination of history. notable works include the performance 'object without shadow,' which examines ghosts in thai-chinese culture, and the film 'and that ocean too is a fiction' (2019). they are co-founder of the center for lost objects (est. 2010). they were a maha pavilion artist at the bangkok biennial 2020 and have undertaken residencies at the singapore art museum. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Song Nguyễn
bio: Nguyễn Song is a Hanoi-based performance artist and core member of Phụ Lục (The Appendix Group), founded in 2010. Phụ Lục served as artists-in-residence throughout the six-month performance plus 2018 programme at MoT+++.

name: Tanya Amador
bio: tanya amador is an independent curator and producer specializing in contemporary art across southeast asia. she is co-founder of peruke projects, a curatorial and production platform, and runs amador arts projects. she holds an MA in curating from goldsmiths, university of london, completed through lasalle college of the arts, singapore. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Thom Nguyen
bio: thom nguyen (b. 1987, ho chi minh city) is a vietnamese-american multidisciplinary artist working across oil painting, sculpture, installation, and fashion. he left vietnam at age four and grew up in the united states, earning a BA in art history from UCLA. his practice explores queerness, diasporic memory, and vietnamese identity — drawing on european portraiture alongside traditional vietnamese forms including ink painting, silk, and seal chops. his solo exhibition nước | water resistance (a.Farm / MoT+++, 2025) centered on the double meaning of the vietnamese word nước — both 'water' and 'nation' — conjuring portraits of queer bodies drawn from vietnamese legends and traditions. based in scituate, massachusetts.

name: Tobias Ahlbrecht
bio: tobias ahlbrecht is a german documentary and fine art photographer. his long-term project DECAY investigates abandoned sites across the former east germany, examining memory and the material traces of the GDR. BA from arts university bournemouth. he exhibited in vietnam in 2019 and participated in MoT+++'s performance plus program.

name: Tran Minh Duc
bio: Trần Minh Đức is a Vietnamese artist whose work flowers is in the +1 museum by any other name collection at MoT+++.

name: Trần Uy Đức
bio: Trần Uy Đức is a Hanoi-based sound and music artist who performed at MoT sound #21 dispari at MoT+++.

name: Tuan Mami
bio: tuan mami (b. 1981, vietnam) is a hanoi-based conceptual artist whose practice spans installation, video, performance, sound, photography, and long-term community-driven projects. his work is often site-specific and reconstructional, addressing questions of art, meaning, and social interaction. a key project, 'in one's breath nothing stands still' (2014–2017), emerged from years of research into pollution, biodiversity loss, and cultural disruption caused by stone mining in his hometown of hà nam. he graduated from hanoi fine art university (2006), founded mac-hanoi (mobile art center) in 2012, and co-founded nha san collective in 2013. his work has been shown at prospect new orleans, framer framed (amsterdam), and kinan art week (japan). he gave an artist presentation during performance plus 2019 at MoT+++.

name: Vũ Đức Toàn
bio: vũ đức toàn (b. 1982, hanoi) is a performance and installation artist and art critic based in hanoi. his practice engages with the relationship between audience and performer, social rituals, and historical contexts. he graduated in art history and criticism from vietnam university of fine arts (2007) and has worked as an editor at the vietnam fine arts association since 2005. in 2010 he co-founded phụ lục (the appendix group) with nguyễn huy an — a six-artist collective known for durational performances with allegorical everyday objects, abstract staging, and minimal gestures addressing the individual and collective. he is a curatorial board member at nhà sàn collective (from 2013) and has performed internationally at future of imagination 8 (singapore), pan asia 5 (seoul), guyu action (china/hong kong/taiwan), and the nguyen art foundation phụ lục project (2025). participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2018 program.

name: Weston Teruya
bio: weston teruya (b. 1977, honolulu) is an oakland-based visual artist and arts administrator whose paper sculptures, installations, and drawings reconfigure symbols to explore power, visibility, protection, and privilege. drawing on material research and the histories of specific communities and neighbourhoods, his sculptural installations — often from paper-based media — examine social dynamics and the textures of place. his 2017 de young museum residency produced 'the city breathing,' a large-scale paper installation. recent collaborative work includes 'who else but americans…,' a photographic sculpture honouring chinese labourers who died building the transcontinental railroad. a 2018 asian cultural council fellow, his work has been shown at the mills college art museum, pro arts gallery, headlands center for the arts, and recology artist residency. he was a resident at a.Farm at MoT+++ in 2019.

name: Wu Chi-Tsung
bio: Wu Chi-Tsung was born in 1981 in Taipei. Wu received his Bachelor of Fine Arts from the Taipei National University of the Arts in 2004. he currently lives and works in Taipei, Taiwan and Berlin, Germany. his practice spans photography, video, installation, painting, and set design, blending Eastern and Western artistic traditions. daily objects serve as inspiration, transformed into poetic imagery. awards include Taipei Arts Award (2003), shortlisted for Artes Mundi (2006), WRO Media Art Biannual Award of Critics and Editors (2013), shortlisted for Prudential Eye Awards (2015), Liu Kuo Sung Ink Art Award (2019).

name: Xxavier Edward Carter
bio: xxavier edward carter (b. 1986, dallas) is a transdisciplinary artist of black and mvskoke descent. originally a football scholar at stanford university, he shifted to studio art, later completing an MFA at southern methodist university. his practice spans books, drawings, films, recordings, paintings, performances, and site-specific installations — often presented as publications and video works. he is head artist and engineer of goldfish dreams, a dallas-based artistic publication and production house. his work has been acquired by the nasher sculpture center (2020) and the dallas museum of art (2022). awards include the arch and anne giles kimbrough award (dma, 2011), freedom fellowship (yerba buena center for the arts, 2016), and the dallas/dijon fellowship (2019). participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Yui Nguyễn
bio: Yui Nguyễn is a Ho Chi Minh City-based artist and co-founder of CodeSurfing (Lướt Code), a creative tech collective that situates technology within artistic, research, and archiving practices through poetical approaches. she presented the talk Codesurfing: A Search for a Poetical Technology at A. Farm in 2025.

name: Zach Sch
bio: zach sch is an experimentally oriented musician and sound artist. classically trained, he moved into experimental music through jazz piano and electronic production, alongside the traditional music of southeast asia. member of the rắn cạp đuôi collective. participated in MoT+++'s performance plus 2019 program.

name: Mr. Bambii
bio: mr. bambii (chinese-vietnamese, b. 1993, ho chi minh city) is a multimedia artist who began as a poet before turning to performance art as a means of living the body of his poetry. wax has become his primary sculpting material — a landscape for visual poetry. as a performance artist he collaborates across disciplines with poets, sound artists, musicians, contemporary dancers, and opera performers. his practice extends to art direction, curation, exhibition production, interior design, set design, and collecting. in 2019 he founded 289e, an underground art space in ho chi minh city that hosted over 100 shows and 15 exhibitions before closing during the pandemic. in 2022 he established ao hõi fine art museum, a foundation dedicated to art conservation. associated with MoT+++ and the a.farm residency.

name: Nguyễn Quốc Chánh
bio: nguyễn quốc chánh (b. 1958, bạc liêu, mekong delta) is a vietnamese poet and ceramic artist whose surreal, alluring ceramics translate the abstract lyricism of his poetry into tactile form. he is the author of four collections of poems — 'night of the rising sun' (1990), 'inanimate weather' (1997), 'coded personal info' (2001), and the samizdat 'hey, i'm here' (2005) — translated into many languages. his ceramic work caught the attention of artist dinh q. lê, who curated 'guerilla tactics' (2018), a solo exhibition of chánh's contemporary ceramics at MoT+++ and sàn art — the first major presentation of his ceramic practice.

name: Dan Nguyen (Demonslayer)
bio: a music producer, DJ, and visual artist dedicated to unearthing and archiving the sights and sounds of Vietnamese New Wave from Little Saigon, California in the 1980s–90s. his +1 contemporary project California Dreaming was presented at MoT+++ and he participated as a guest artist during A. Farm season 4.

name: Tuyp Tran
bio: Tuyp Tran (Tran Quoc Huy, b. 1988) is a self-trained Vietnamese visual artist whose oeuvre encompasses food, sex, and spirituality. his intricate multilayered paint and ink compositions connect seemingly disparate subjects through their shared relation to the human psyche. he presented exhibitions at MoT+++ including Psyche (2016) and Persona (2017).

name: Phùng Tiến Sơn
bio: a Hanoi-based artist whose practice engages with music composition and technology. he presented Soundtracks (Ký Âm), a sound installation at MoT+++ running from February to April 2019, building on his history as a musical composer and his interest in the digital manipulation of sound.

name: Laurent Weyl
bio: a French photographer whose work engages with social and political realities. he presented President Hotel, a solo exhibition and book launch at MoT+++ in 2017, documenting the Hotel President — an imposing structure built for the US army in 1970s Saigon — before its demolition.

name: Pamela N. Corey
bio: an art historian and professor whose research focuses on contemporary Vietnamese art. she was hosted by A. Farm for a reading and discussion of her essay The (Calli)graphic Regimes of Contemporary Vietnamese Art (published on Artmargins, 2024).

name: MAP Office
bio: a Hong Kong-based interdisciplinary practice founded by Laurent Gutierrez and Valérie Portefaix in 1996. their work unfolds across territories, exploring unstable global geographies through research, publications, and installations. they presented Islands, Archipelagos and Other Liquid Territories at MoT+++ in 2015.

name: Régis Golay
bio: a Swiss photographer born in Geneva (1978), graduate of the Art and Design College of Lausanne. based at Federal Studio, Geneva, his work engages with architecture, labor, and the built environment. he presented Architecture as a Theater at MoT+++ in collaboration with group8asia (2015) and the duo show Figures, Forms, Contraptions with Thy Tran (2017).

name: Baby Reni (Irene Ha)
bio: irene ha (b. 1997) works under the name baby reni, interrogating fashion conventions through an art practice shaped by her experience as a second-generation vietnamese immigrant in the netherlands. growing up with few southeast asian role models in her immediate world, she draws from internet culture and the spaces between western fashion and traditional vietnamese dress. her collections navigate hybrid identity, systemic collaboration, and the aesthetics of a generation raised online. she graduated from the fashion department at gerrit rietveld academie in amsterdam (2020) and was a finalist in lichting 2020. her work has been shown at unfair amsterdam, non native native fair, and diez gallery. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Enkhbold Togmidshiirev
bio: enkhbold togmidshiirev (b. 1978) is a mongolian contemporary artist whose installations and performances question the divisions between natural and urban landscapes. raised in the mongolian countryside before moving to ulaanbaatar as a student, his practice interrogates the barriers humans construct between themselves and nature. since 2009 he has travelled with a portable ger (traditional dwelling) of his own construction, setting it up in cities, beaches, and roadsides across finland, the netherlands, and the uk. in 2015 he represented mongolia at the 56th venice biennale — mongolia's first-ever participation — presenting a nomadic pavilion that wandered through the giardini. his work has been shown at the 9th asia pacific triennial (brisbane). he was a resident artist during performance plus 2018 at MoT+++.

name: Lập Xuân
bio: Lập Xuân (Do Nguyen Lap-Xuan) is a Ho Chi Minh City-based artist who participated in performance plus 2019 at MoT+++.

name: Lem Trag
bio: lem trag is a visual artist of german-vietnamese heritage. growing up between germany and vietnam, the intertwined state of eastern-western spectacles and cultures creates rich and surreal nuances in her work. she does not limit herself to a particular medium — for her, artmaking is a space of discovery through a creative process characterised by simultaneity and experimentation. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Karen-Thao Nguyen La
bio: karen-thao nguyen la is a multi-disciplinary artist and designer from vancouver, canada. she relocated to ho chi minh city to explore her diasporic lineage. her practice occupies the liminal space between hyphenated identity, with physically immersive research rooted in commonplace rituals of public and private spheres — work, leisure, and the spaces in between. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Alisa Chunchue
bio: alisa chunchue has a keen interest in the human body as testimony to relationships, livelihood, and social environment. many of her works draw on medical research, chemistry, science, and calculation. she emphasises the process of physical exploration and the essence of materials. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Tuyen Nguyen
bio: for tuyen nguyen, the studio is an intimate subject that provides his artistic experiments with emotion. his paintings are often distorted, deformed, and observed from various viewpoints at different times and places to produce new sensations and imaginations. he wishes to express internal depth rather than external form. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Scott Farrand
bio: scott farrand is an emerging artist from adelaide, south australia. he holds a bachelor of visual art and design from the adelaide college of the arts, majoring in sculpture. his painting and sculptural works express an ongoing interest in values derived from the natural world. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Cora von Zezschwitz & Tilman Hoepfl
bio: cora von zezschwitz and tilman hoepfl live and work in france. alongside their individual practices — photography, video, installation, and painting/sculpture — they have collaborated since 2016 on projects exploring social sculpture. based on modular structures and the idea of architecture as addition, they create physical platforms for communal engagement. they were residents at a.farm.

name: Scott Anderson
bio: scott anderson is an australian artist whose practice explores painting, collage, and sculpture as a hybridised whole. his research investigates architecture, place and non-place, transition, construction and deconstruction, and how cultural identities influence one's understanding of place. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Mariana Tubio-Blasio
bio: mariana tubio-blasio is a writer and researcher from brazil. during her residency she documented the projects and creative processes of artists at a.farm and in the region, with particular interest in new expressions and cultural production in vietnam. her work contributed to amor fati, a documentary exploring the bias of art and creativity. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Annabelle Yep
bio: annabelle yep is an emerging multidisciplinary artist from hong kong, based in new york city. she holds a BA in sociology from skidmore college. her work combines interdisciplinary interests in phenomenology, sociology, and critical theory to explore embodiment, borders, desire, and abjection. influenced by abstract expressionism, textual abstraction, and feminist thought. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Narelle Zhao
bio: narelle zhao is an emerging contemporary artist working across metalwork, sculpture, video, photography, and drawing. born and raised in shanghai, they earned a BS in philosophy and art from skidmore college and are based in new york city. their multidisciplinary practice allows them to construct intricate, material-based processes. they were a resident at a.farm.

name: Linh San
bio: linh san is a multidisciplinary artist based in hanoi, vietnam. she holds a bachelor's degree in literature from hanoi national university of education and works across poetry, moving images, and ceramics. her work depicts poetic, plain, and contemplative moments from daily life. her literary works often take the form of prose poetry — a reflection of her way of thinking. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Damon Đức Phạm
bio: damon đức phạm works with language, sound, and digital rendering. his recent interests include pleasure, cruelty, interiority, public space, and ambience. his audio artworks appear on music platforms as 'especially.' he grew up in california's central valley and currently lives in hồ chí minh city. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Mascha Serga
bio: mascha serga (b. 1997) is a ukrainian-german artist who studied at udk berlin under valérie favre. her works range from playgrounds to war, dominance, hunting, and violence. she has been nominated for the stiftung des deutschen volkes scholarship and received the cusanuswerk scholarship. her works have been shown at galerie c, galerie weserhalle, and künstlerhaus bethanien. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Blake Palmer
bio: blake palmer is a scholar, writer, and cultural critic based in chiang mai, thailand, working primarily on asian contemporary art, particularly east and southeast asia. his interests centre on the intersection of culture, power, and art as a vector of sociopolitical critique. his academic research at chiang mai university focuses on multispecies biopolitics. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: David Willis
bio: david willis is a writer, curator, and art advisor currently based in lisbon, portugal. he holds an MFA in art criticism and writing from the school of visual arts and a BA in cultural anthropology from columbia university. after launching his career at gagosian and pace in new york city, he spent a decade in vietnam and thailand, developing a specialization in southeast asian contemporary art. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Laura Philips
bio: laura philips is a dutch musician, writer, poet, and artist whose work explores identity, loneliness, love, and other universal themes. winding through multiple disciplines, she searches for ways to express and create intuitively. 'entering unknown territory is impressively freeing as you have no assumptions, expectations, or limitations.' she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Anh Vo
bio: anh vo is a vietnamese choreographer and writer based in brooklyn, ny. described by the new york times as 'risky, erotic, enigmatic and boldly humorous,' their works flesh out the tremulous sexual body as a vessel for apparitional forces. their latest body of work attempts to communicate the monotonous oppressiveness that is the weather of postwar contemporary vietnam. they were a resident at a.farm.

name: Châu Kim Sanh
bio: châu kim sanh is a choreographer-dancer, filmmaker, and cultural worker. her stage creations have been presented at the mai, tangente, l'arsenal, l'écart (canada), krossing-over (vietnam), performance curator initiatives (philippines), and sidance (korea). she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Espen Iden
bio: espen iden's practice is inspired by ordinary interpersonal encounters and social relationships. he creates new spaces for collaboration and works concurrently with painting, photography, music, education, and curation. he looks towards the richness of the fantasy world as much as established subcultures. his visual work consists of oil paintings and analog photography. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Bert Ackley
bio: bert ackley is a visual artist and amateur archivist based in ho chi minh city. he created and runs the vinatapes project, an archive of dated vietnamese media focusing on home videos recorded on analog camcorders. he prepares visual work using media and gear found at junk markets, employing crt tvs and a 1990s video mixer for various feedback effects. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Nghia Dang
bio: nghia dang is a saigon-based artist born in 1994 in hanoi. he holds a BA in multi-disciplinary studio practices from the school of the art institute of chicago. his practice is influenced by jungian and lacanian psychological concepts, exploring themes of the self and its interactions with 'others,' as well as family and displacement. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Chu Hao Pei
bio: chu hao pei (b. 1990) is a visual artist born and based in singapore. trained in interactive media at the school of art, design & media at nanyang technological university, his practice is informed by shifting ecological, social, and urban landscapes. by interweaving documentation and intervention, he explores conflicts between development and nature. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Ly Trang
bio: ly trang is a sound designer and composer exploring artistic versatility and alternative approaches to creativity. she creates sounds using technology and traditional instruments, producing work rich in texture, progress, exploration, and singularity. her practice opens the door to the illusion of sound as environment. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Saverio Tonoli
bio: saverio tonoli (b. 1984, italy) crosses different techniques in his painting practice — ink brushwork, fresco techniques, and processes on various papers. he grew up in the darkroom, observing the behaviours of liquids and chemicals on images, which he translated into painting over the years. during his stay at a.farm he developed new work inspired by vietnamese landscapes. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Yeonjeong
bio: yeonjeong works with installation, photography, sound, text, and interdisciplinary mediums experimentally, with particular focus on performance and video. she aims to make artistic interventions in ordinary life and bring out social and cultural issues critically and humorously. she has had solo shows in thailand, taiwan, and south korea and participates in international performance festivals. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Claire Bloomfield
bio: claire bloomfield is a new media artist originally from the usa, currently residing in da nang, vietnam. she is a curator and co-founder of mineral house media, an online digital residency program, and a traditional tantra yoga teacher. she combines painting, sculpture, and photography to create tantalising works that invite stillness and contemplation. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Kayla Kurin
bio: kayla kurin is a health, travel, and fiction writer from canada. she is the author of the bestselling yoga for chronic illness series and 'where can i find wifi? work anywhere, travel forever: tales of a digital nomad.' she has been featured on matador network, yoga international, tiny buddha, and more. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Aram Han Sifuentes
bio: aram han sifuentes is a fiber and performance artist who works to claim spaces for immigrant and disenfranchised communities. her work often revolves around skill sharing, specifically sewing techniques, creating multiethnic and intergenerational sewing circles as sites of empowerment, subversion, and protest. her works have been exhibited at pulitzer arts foundation (st. louis), among others. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Roberto Sifuentes
bio: roberto sifuentes is an interdisciplinary performance artist and educator. recent projects include #exsanguination (2016–2019), a collaboration with new media artist joncates and aram han sifuentes; and 'reculmulations: digital avatars and performance objects,' a collaboration with digital artist claudia hart and composer edmund campion (black and white gallery nyc). he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Nguyen Le Phuong Linh
bio: born in vietnam, nguyen le phuong linh relocated to france to study at the école de beaux-arts de nîmes, graduating in 2016. based in the gard region in the south of france, she works as a photographer drawing inspiration from landscapes, ever-evolving natural terrain, and the rituals of everyday life. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Levi Masuli
bio: a writer by profession, levi masuli used to play jazz piano in hotels and cafes before exploring more ways to create sound. he now investigates its conceptual possibilities. with hyperproduction and saturation defining the current creative zeitgeist, he aims not just to create more sound but to challenge existing approaches. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Kanich Khajohnsri
bio: kanich khajohnsri's practice focuses on space, demarcations, and overlaps between spaces. through observations in bangkok, he has noticed the ambiguity resulting from overlaps between private and public spaces. his research explores the ambiguity of physical space — landscape, area, place — and the boundaries between them. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Rachel TonThat
bio: rachel tonthat is a film photographer, multimedia artist, and fiction writer living in hanoi, vietnam. influenced by her childhood dream of becoming an explorer, her photographic work documents moments from the ongoing story of climate change and human development. her stories draw from folk tales, myth, and oral storytelling. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Tina Thu
bio: tina thu is a chinese-vietnamese artist born in long khanh, vietnam. she immigrated to lawrence, massachusetts at three months old and studied printmaking at massachusetts college of art and design. inspired by her family's practice of ancestral veneration, her work illustrates childhood dreams of hellscapes through the process of mapping imaginary lands. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Matteo Biella
bio: matteo biella graduated as an architect from tu delft, the netherlands, in 2015, and his practice has blended into art. he recycles wood from old and broken skateboard decks to create artworks inspired by his architectural research. he has undertaken residencies and presented works in chengdu, shanghai, sapporo, and tokyo. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Edén Barrena
bio: edén barrena (b. 1991, badajoz, spain) is a visual artist working with drawing. she holds a fine arts BA from universidad complutense de madrid and a printmaking MFA from the royal college of art, london. she studied at staatliche akademie der bildenden künste stuttgart on an erasmus scholarship. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Constance Meffre
bio: trained in art history and cultural & exhibition management, constance meffre has also studied visual arts and music in france, the netherlands, and japan. in 2010 she founded d.d.a (diffusing digital art), a curatorial and artistic platform for new media and sound art in marseille. she develops activities to support artists with production, curatorial exhibitions, and sound-based projects. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: z1 studio
bio: combining personal experiences and research in sound, light, and space, camille de dieu and laurent novac created studio z1, a digital performative studio, in 2016. over the last decade, camille de dieu has focused on materials and their reaction to context using projection mapping, 3d modelling, sculpture, and light projection. laurent novac works with sound and performance. they were residents at a.farm.

name: Natalia Ludmila
bio: natalia ludmila is an artist working at the intersection of painting, drawing, video, and sound. her studio-based research centres on problematising the portrayal of social issues, questioning forms of representation and the construction of false or biased narratives. she looks for the possibility of different and multiple readings. she was a resident at a.farm.

name: Alex Williams (Pug)
bio: pug (alex williams) is a transnational multidisciplinary artist from djillang/geelong, australia. his practice centres on the collection and transformation of reclaimed materials into immersive sound sculptural installations. his work delves into the ritualistic and transformative potential of materials, creating environments that foster curiosity, play, and speculative thought. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Lau Wang Tat
bio: born in hong kong, lau wang tat holds a BA in fine art co-presented by hong kong art school and rmit university, and an MA in fine arts from the chinese university of hong kong. in 2018 he participated in the artist residency at the shigaraki ceramic cultural park, japan. combining ceramics, sculpture, and installation, his work explores material transformation. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Vicente Arrese
bio: vicente arrese (b. 1993, santiago, chile) works primarily with painting and explores the potential of everyday things — eggs, washing machines, cars. fascinated by the endless scenarios an object can generate and the intersections with other disciplines, he invites audiences to question conventional perception through sculptural language and spatial arrangement. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Dương Tử Quế
bio: dương tử quế is a danang-born, saigon-based image-maker and independent art organiser. his practice focuses on personal adaptation and projection of images and symbols, primarily through photography and other media that function by storing and visualizing the unconscious, while validating mechanisms for tracing (pseudo)scientific grounds. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Nguyễn Hoá
bio: nguyễn hoá works primarily with sculpture, woodcarving, lacquer, and painting. having close ties with his hometown, huế, he converses with the past of the nguyen dynasty and its legacy — temples, mausoleums, traditions, and rituals. his works weave together woodworking techniques, traditional lacquer, and distilled forms of painting. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Duy Nguyen
bio: duy nguyen (b. 1989, kuala lumpur, malaysia) grew up in the bidong refugee camp and migrated to norway as a child, spending his youth on the west coast. with a background in design, he has developed an image-based art practice exploring themes of migration, diaspora stories, and multicultural identity — often in connection with mental health. he was a resident at a.farm.

name: Lan Anh Lê
bio: for lananh, artmaking was a psychological healing tool and meditative process, linked to ancestral worship and the search for spiritual order in a world of chaos. her practice lay between multimedia experimentation and art therapy, aiming to heal cynical disillusions through fantastical reveries and magical escapisms. she was a resident at a.farm. (1993–2020)

name: Latthapon Korkiatarkul
bio: latthapon korkiatarkul is a bangkok-based conceptual artist whose process detaches from conventional structures of drawing, painting, and sculpture. he reverses sequences of production, sometimes painting on the reverse of canvases or soaking paper in oil before turning to color. his practice includes seemingly inconsequential remnants like paintbrush hair, pencil shavings, and dust. a graduate of bangkok university school of fine and applied art (2010), he has exhibited in bangkok, sydney, singapore, and hong kong. he was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Luca Lum
bio: luca lum is a singapore-born artist and writer working at the intersection of performance, poetry, and fiction. her work explores language, vulnerability, intimacy, mourning, and the phenomenology of the event. co-founder of artist-run space soft/WALL/studs (2016–2021) and co-editor of CONCRETE ISLAND Reader. she has been artist-in-residence at delfina foundation (uk), ntu centre for contemporary art (sg), and cemeti institute (id). she was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Bagus Mazasupa Anwarridwan
bio: bagus mazasupa anwarridwan is a pianist and composer from yogyakarta, indonesia. trained in classical piano at institut seni indonesia yogyakarta, his career developed through projects including the band sirkus barock and theater group saturday acting club. in 2017, he started musikono, a project bringing solo piano concerts to rural villages across indonesia. he was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Ben Valentine
bio: ben valentine is an independent writer and curator studying how technology, media, and politics intersect around the world. his writing has appeared in hyperallergic, salon, sfaq, and the new inquiry. his research focuses particularly on southeast asia, including vietnam and cambodia. he was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Maung Day
bio: maung day is a myanmar-born poet, editor, and visual and performance artist based in bangkok. he has published eight books of poetry in burmese and one chapbook in english. his work delivers defiant social commentary on myanmar's past and present through prose poems and ink drawings. he has exhibited in bangkok, germany, austria, new zealand, hong kong, and vietnam. he was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Tricia Nguyen Thanh Trang
bio: tricia nguyen is a vietnamese artist and curator, daughter of artist nguyen thanh binh. she created wintercearig, an exhibition and workshop series focusing on mental health awareness through art in vietnam. her practice includes choreography, directing, and collaborative work with artists exploring themes of vulnerability and community. she was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Tram Luong
bio: tram (carin) luong is a lens-based multimedia artist, anthropologist, and educator based in ho chi minh city. her work is situated at the intersection of anthropology, visual arts, and public advocacy, taking a research-based approach to artistic mediation. she was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Nguyen Duc Phuong
bio: nguyen duc phuong is a vietnamese artist working with natural materials like soil, water, and paper. his practice draws on journeys across traditional craft villages in north vietnam, reflecting local nuances and folk spirit. his work has been exhibited at nguyen art foundation, the factory contemporary arts center, vcca hanoi, and nha san collective. he was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: John Edmond Smyth
bio: john edmond smyth is an irish artist who was an a. farm resident in season 0 (2018).

name: Nguyễn Thúy-Hằng
bio:
Nguyễn Thúy-Hằng (b. 1978, Ho Chi Minh City) is a Vietnamese artist and writer whose practice bridges literature and visual art. Her debut trilogy — Current Times, Good Sensations, and Reasonable Insanity — written during two years in the United States, was published in Hanoi in 2006 and became a cultural phenomenon.

Her paintings and sculptures echo the sentiments of her writing, while her fiction gives visual ideas cryptic new form through text. Drawing on life in post-war Vietnam and an education spanning the US and Vietnam, her work diverges from socialist realism to confront hidden societal taboos — deconstructing the psychology of different social groups through structures of fantasy and introspection.

Selected exhibitions: Land(e)scape, Galerie BAO, Paris (2023); Histoire d'un voyage, The Factory, HCMC (2017); The Third Kunming Art Biennale, China (2016); SHE, Ugly Duck Gallery, London (2016); The Warriors, Saigon Domaine (2015); The Walker, British Council & City Fine Arts Museum, HCMC (2013).
